- @solana’s RWA ecosystem has reached a new all-time high of ~$2 billion in total value.

- @SolanaEvents One month to Solana Accelerate, May 5th in Miami

- @SuiNetwork SUI is now live on solana via @Sunrise_DeFi

- @UmbraPrivacy launched their privacy centered wallet on IOS only currently and released their SDK for developers

- @magicblock unveiled their Private Payments API is now. Add confidential SPL transfers into your app in minutes with one API call

- @phantom announced you can now track Profit and Loss (P&L) for your Solana tokens, right on Phantom App.

- @perena launched two new ways to hold USD STAR (USD*) -> USD* Junior and USD* Protected

- @SolanaFndn has joined the x402 Foundation at @linuxfoundation

- @soilonic is bringing Soil as an asset on solana!

- SBI Holdings' B2C2 designates Solana as its primary network for institutional stablecoin settlement

tweets.full_text:
Say what you want, but Solana is the most resilient chain on earth.

We’ve been stress tested harder than anyone.

Outages, fud, bear markets, the FTX collapse, scams, hacks, and endless criticism.

We’re the lab rats of web3 innovation.

That means many failures, but we also get the 10000x breakthroughs.

Most ecosystems would have folded by now.

We keep building. We keep improving. We keep showing up.

We’ve made mistakes, learned from them, and came back stronger every single time.

That kind of resilience is difficult to beat, and that’s exactly why we’ll win.

tweets.full_text:
🚨 Drift Protocol Hack — Key Update (Apr 3, 2026)
One of the largest DeFi exploits this year has hit Solana.

▫️~$285M drained (est. $270–286M)
▫️ >50% TVL wiped in ~12 minutes
▫️ Assets stolen: USDC, SOL, JLP, WBTC & more
▫️ 2nd largest hack in Solana history (after Wormhole)

 ⚠️ Attack vector (not a code exploit):
The attacker leveraged durable nonces — a legitimate Solana feature — to execute pre-signed admin transactions prepared weeks in advance.
→ Gained control of the Security Council (multisig)
→ Removed withdrawal limits
→ Drained protocol vaults at high speed

👉 Post-exploit flow:
▫️ Funds bridged to Ethereum via Circle’s CCTP
▫️~129K ETH moved cross-chain

🔎 Notable developments:
▫️ Suspected DPRK/Lazarus Group involvement (pattern-based attribution)
▫️Criticism toward Circle for delayed USDC freeze (~6h)
▫️Contagion impact: 10+ Solana protocols affected
▫️ $DRIFT token dropped ~30–37% at lows

📍 Current status:
 ▫️Investigation ongoing
 ▫️ No confirmed fund recovery yet
 ▫️Solana core infra unaffected — but DeFi trust takes a hit.

🧩 This wasn’t a smart contract bug — it was a governance + key management failure.
Durable nonces + compromised admin flow = catastrophic risk.

⚠️Stay cautious. Monitor trusted sources like Arkham, PeckShield, Elliptic, and @DriftProtocol for updates.

result.text:
🚨 @DriftProtocol was hit by a major exploit on April 1, with ~$270–285M drained from vaults within ~1 hour via suspicious transactions.

Initial findings suggest the attacker leveraged pre-signed durable nonce transactions combined with possible social engineering (rather than a typical smart contract bug), moving funds across chains to Ethereum to obfuscate flows.

Drift has paused deposits/withdrawals, confirmed an active attack, and is working with security partners and exchanges to contain damage while investigations continue — users are advised to revoke approvals and stay alert as the situation develops.

- Duplicate mutable accounts rejected by default

tweets.full_text:
🚨 @DriftProtocol hack update
The team confirms this was a 6-month coordinated social engineering campaign, not a smart contract exploit—attackers posed as a trading firm, built trust across IRL meetings + onchain activity, then compromised contributors via malicious repos & fake wallet apps.

Drift has fully frozen the protocol, removed compromised wallets from multisig, and is working with security firms & law enforcement while publicly sharing details to help the ecosystem learn—urging all Solana teams to treat every multisig device as a target and audit access immediately.

The attack is linked with medium-high confidence to Lazarus Group–style operations, reinforcing that human-layer security is now DeFi’s biggest risk vector. ⚠️

👉 Anatoly Yakovenko noted Drift handled loss socialization fairly, suggesting a path forward: rebuild with a lean core team + compensate users via structured recovery (e.g. airdrops).

⚠️ Takeaway: this isn’t just a Drift incident—it’s a wake-up call for all Solana projects to harden opsec, isolate signer devices, and rethink trust in offchain interactions.

result.text:
🚨 Drift Protocol Hack — Key Update (Apr 3, 2026)
One of the largest DeFi exploits this year has hit Solana.

▫️~$285M drained (est. $270–286M)
▫️ >50% TVL wiped in ~12 minutes
▫️ Assets stolen: USDC, SOL, JLP, WBTC & more
▫️ 2nd largest hack in Solana history (after Wormhole)

 ⚠️ Attack vector (not a code exploit):
The attacker leveraged durable nonces — a legitimate Solana feature — to execute pre-signed admin transactions prepared weeks in advance.
→ Gained control of the Security Council (multisig)
→ Removed withdrawal limits
→ Drained protocol vaults at high speed

👉 Post-exploit flow:
▫️ Funds bridged to Ethereum via Circle’s CCTP
▫️~129K ETH moved cross-chain

🔎 Notable developments:
▫️ Suspected DPRK/Lazarus Group involvement (pattern-based attribution)
▫️Criticism toward Circle for delayed USDC freeze (~6h)
▫️Contagion impact: 10+ Solana protocols affected
▫️ $DRIFT token dropped ~30–37% at lows

📍 Current status:
 ▫️Investigation ongoing
 ▫️ No confirmed fund recovery yet
 ▫️Solana core infra unaffected — but DeFi trust takes a hit.

🧩 This wasn’t a smart contract bug — it was a governance + key management failure.
Durable nonces + compromised admin flow = catastrophic risk.

⚠️Stay cautious. Monitor trusted sources like Arkham, PeckShield, Elliptic, and @DriftProtocol for updates.

At Jupiter, our focus remains on safety above all else.

And while the protocol is open sourced, audited 7x, and formally verified, code controls are only one layer.

That's why we wanted to give you full transparency into Jupiter Lend's operational security as well.

Here's what's in place:

• All program upgrades require 4/7 signers, and are subject to a 12 hour timelock

• All non-program upgrades require 6/10 signers

• All multisig transactions require at least 3 human verifications of the underlying txn construction

In addition to this, Fluid's architecture has a built-in circuit breaker via its automated borrow limits. These limits expand gradually to prevent the rapid draining of assets and are hard-capped at a maximum rate that can never be crossed.

We don't ask you to take our word for it. You can verify our audits and multisigs from our transparency dashboard here: https://t.co/545pIK0yXX

At Jupiter, our focus remains on safety above all else.

And while the protocol is open sourced, audited 7x, and formally verified, code controls are only one layer.

That's why we wanted to give you full transparency into Jupiter Lend's operational security as well.

Here's what's in place:

• All program upgrades require 4/7 signers, and are subject to a 12 hour timelock

• All non-program upgrades require 6/10 signers

• All multisig transactions require at least 3 human verifications of the underlying txn construction

In addition to this, Fluid's architecture has a built-in circuit breaker via its automated borrow limits. These limits expand gradually to prevent the rapid draining of assets and are hard-capped at a maximum rate that can never be crossed.

We don't ask you to take our word for it. You can verify our audits and multisigs from our transparency dashboard here: https://t.co/545pIK0yXX

burnie-sol, $125, spot -20% on the first pump. quick in, quick out.

the entry was on a strong pump with huge volume coming in. that's exactly when spot makes sense. you're not chasing, you're placing your range right where the price is heading and letting the volume fill your bins.

this pool still decent to farm for multidays : https://t.co/D7bNQE8wpV

went OOR for a bit. didn't touch it.

the first wave exit pump almost always comes if you entered on a real runner with solid volume. patience is the strat here, not chart watching every 30 seconds.

the honest take though: this position would've been more profitable if i deployed bid-ask and flipped it instead. 

spot -20% captured the move but bid-ask on the same token with the same volume would've caught fees in both directions. still $125 is decent for this market condition.

if you see a token with a strong first pump and volume coming in fast, spot is the easier and cleaner entry. you don't need to overthink the range setup, you just need to be patient enough to wait for the exit wave.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy
